question: How do hidden product-space dimensions and Pixie intervention depth change the visibility, sequestration, and resurfacing of deterministic critter responses?
experimental_unit: one independently seeded paired treated-versus-untreated folded-cavern episode

hypotheses.prediction: For fixed-degree worlds at dimensions 4-11, axis probes produce a visible resurfacing event in at least 75% of fresh episodes for at least two critters.
hypotheses.falsifier: Fewer than two critters reach 75% resurfacing occupancy on confirmation or holdout.

hypotheses.prediction: Fiber-column actions have at least as high mean peak visible-slice divergence as surface-local actions for every critter on each fresh split.
hypotheses.falsifier: Any critter has lower fiber-column mean peak visible divergence on confirmation or holdout.

hypotheses.prediction: No preferred-action fixed-degree episode reaches 75% full-world categorical divergence.
Response class depends on intervention depth with mutual information above 0.10 bits on both fresh splits.
hypotheses.falsifier: Depth/response-class mutual information is at most 0.10 bits on confirmation or holdout.

model.update_order: At declared ticks the preferred species action is applied at the declared depth mask, then treated and exact untreated worlds receive the same synchronous substrate update.
model.rng: numpy.default_rng(seed) initializes paired worlds identically; no randomness is consumed after initialization.

seed_plan.pairing: Treated and comparator are exact initialization copies; only the declared Pixie intervention differs.

denominators.full_divergent_fraction: categorically divergent treated/comparator sites divided by all product-space sites
denominators.visible_divergent_fraction: divergent sites on hidden-coordinate-zero divided by 64 visible sites
denominators.hidden_divergent_fraction: divergent off-surface sites divided by all off-surface sites
denominators.hidden_response_share: off-surface divergent sites divided by all divergent sites

exposure.eligibility: Every condition constructs a nonempty, deterministic intervention mask.
exposure.activation: Every episode attempts the species-preferred action at ticks 6, 12, and 18.
exposure.state_change: Exact pre-display substrate differences record immediate changes; later categorical response is separate.

analysis.uncertainty: Episode-level occupancy intervals and deterministic episode bootstrap intervals; sites and ticks are not replicates.
analysis.multiple_comparisons: All declared geometry/depth cells are retained; hypotheses aggregate frozen contrasts rather than selecting a champion.
analysis.stopping_rule: Stop after 288 episodes, 300 wall seconds, 1024 MB RSS, 100 MB artifacts, failed exposure, or failed exact replay.

budget.execution: Sequential local CPU execution with no external model calls.
